D7 Print L1 Identify Atom

Newsprint Under the Loupe

a printed photo is not grey ink — it is solid black dots of varying size that the eye blends into tone at arm's length

A press lays down one ink at full strength — it cannot print "40% grey". Instead it breaks the image into a fine grid of black dots whose size grows in the shadows and shrinks in the highlights. Up close you see the dots; from a normal viewing distance they fuse into continuous tone.

Slide the screen ruling from coarse newsprint to fine magazine stock. Coarse dots are obvious; fine ones almost vanish. Either way the dots are pure black — only their area carries the greyscale.
